NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. The heavens declare the glory of God; And the firmament shows His handiwork.
NKJV.2: Day unto day utters speech, And night unto night reveals knowledge.
NKJV.3: There is no speech nor language Where their voice is not heard.
NKJV.4: Their line has gone out through all the earth, And their words to the end of the world. In them He has set a tabernacle for the sun,
NKJV.5: Which is like a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, And rejoices like a strong man to run its race.
NKJV.6: Its rising is from one end of heaven, And its circuit to the other end; And there is nothing hidden from its heat.
NKJV.7: The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul; The testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple;
NKJV.8: The statutes of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart; The commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes;
NKJV.9: The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; The judgments of the LORD are true and righteous altogether.
NKJV.10: More to be desired are they than gold, Yea, than much fine gold; Sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.
NKJV.11: Moreover by them Your servant is warned, And in keeping them there is great reward.
NKJV.12: Who can understand his errors? Cleanse me from secret faults.
NKJV.13: Keep back Your servant also from presumptuous sins; Let them not have dominion over me. Then I shall be blameless, And I shall be innocent of great transgression.
NKJV.14: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be acceptable in Your sight, O LORD, my strength and my Redeemer.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. The heavens<S>8064</S> declare<S>5608</S> the glory<S>3519</S> of God<S>410</S>; and the firmament<S>7549</S> sheweth his<S>5046</S> handywork<S>4639</S> <S>3027</S>.

KJV.5: Which is as a bridegroom<S>2860</S> coming out<S>3318</S> of his chamber<S>2646</S>, and rejoiceth<S>7797</S> as a strong man<S>1368</S> to run<S>7323</S> a race<S>734</S>.
KJV.6: His going forth<S>4161</S> is from the end<S>7097</S> of the heaven<S>8064</S>, and his circuit<S>8622</S> unto the ends<S>7098</S> of it: and there is nothing hid<S>5641</S> from the heat<S>2535</S> thereof.
KJV.7: The law<S>8451</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is perfect<S>8549</S>, converting<S>7725</S> the soul<S>5315</S>: the testimony<S>5715</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is sure<S>539</S>, making wise<S>2449</S> the simple<S>6612</S>. <sup>law: or, doctrine</sup> <sup>converting: or, restoring</sup>
KJV.8: The statutes<S>6490</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> are right<S>3477</S>, rejoicing<S>8055</S> the heart<S>3820</S>: the commandment<S>4687</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is pure<S>1249</S>, enlightening<S>215</S> the eyes<S>5869</S>.
KJV.9: The fear<S>3374</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is clean<S>2889</S>, enduring<S>5975</S> for ever<S>5703</S>: the judgments<S>4941</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> are true<S>571</S> and righteous<S>6663</S> altogether<S>3162</S>. <sup>true: Heb. truth</sup>
KJV.10: More to be desired<S>2530</S> are they than gold<S>2091</S>, yea, than much<S>7227</S> fine gold<S>6337</S>: sweeter<S>4966</S> also than honey<S>1706</S> and the honeycomb<S>5317</S> <S>6688</S>. <sup>the honeycomb: Heb. the dropping of honeycombs</sup>
KJV.11: Moreover by them is thy servant<S>5650</S> warned<S>2094</S>: and in keeping<S>8104</S> of them there is great<S>7227</S> reward<S>6118</S>.
KJV.12: Who can understand<S>995</S> his errors<S>7691</S>? cleanse<S>5352</S> thou me from secret<S>5641</S> faults.
KJV.13: Keep back<S>2820</S> thy servant<S>5650</S> also from presumptuous<S>2086</S> sins; let them not have dominion<S>4910</S> over me: then shall I be upright<S>8552</S>, and I shall be innocent<S>5352</S> from the great<S>7227</S> transgression<S>6588</S>. <sup>the great: or, much</sup>
KJV.14: Let the words<S>561</S> of my mouth<S>6310</S>, and the meditation<S>1902</S> of my heart<S>3820</S>, be acceptable<S>7522</S> in thy sight<S>6440</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, my strength<S>6697</S>, and my redeemer<S>1350</S>. <sup>strength: Heb. rock</sup>

ESV.1: The heavens declare the glory of God, and the sky above  proclaims his handiwork.
ESV.2: Day to day pours out speech, and night to night reveals knowledge.
ESV.3: There is no speech, nor are there words, whose voice is not heard.
ESV.4: Their voice  goes out through all the earth, and their words to the end of the world. In them he has set a tent for the sun,
ESV.5: which comes out like a bridegroom leaving his chamber, and, like a strong man, runs its course with joy.
ESV.6: Its rising is from the end of the heavens, and its circuit to the end of them, and there is nothing hidden from its heat.
ESV.7: The law of the LORD is perfect,  reviving the soul; the testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple;
ESV.8: the precepts of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes;
ESV.9: the fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; the rules  of the LORD are true, and righteous altogether.
ESV.10: More to be desired are they than gold, even much fine gold; sweeter also than honey and drippings of the honeycomb.
ESV.11: Moreover, by them is your servant warned; in keeping them there is great reward.
ESV.12: Who can discern his errors? Declare me innocent from hidden faults.
ESV.13: Keep back your servant also from presumptuous sins; let them not have dominion over me! Then I shall be blameless, and innocent of great transgression.
ESV.14: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight, O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. The heavens are telling of the glory of God; And their expanse is declaring the work of His hands.
NASB.2: Day to day pours forth speech, And night to night reveals knowledge.
NASB.3: There is no speech, nor are there words; Their voice is not heard.
NASB.4: Their line has gone out through all the earth, And their utterances to the end of the world. In them He has placed a tent for the sun,
NASB.5: Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber; It rejoices as a strong man to run his course.
NASB.6: Its rising is from one end of the heavens, And its circuit to the other end of them; And there is nothing hidden from its heat.
NASB.7: The law of the LORD is perfect, restoring the soul; The testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple.
NASB.8: The precepts of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart; The commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.
NASB.9: The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; The judgments of the LORD are true; they are righteous altogether.
NASB.10: They are more desirable than gold, yes, than much fine gold; Sweeter also than honey and the drippings of the honeycomb.
NASB.11: Moreover, by them Your servant is warned; In keeping them there is great reward.
NASB.12: Who can discern [his] errors? Acquit me of hidden [faults].
NASB.13: Also keep back Your servant from presumptuous [sins]; Let them not rule over me; Then I will be blameless, And I shall be acquitted of great transgression.
NASB.14: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be acceptable in Your sight, O LORD, my rock and my Redeemer.

NLT.1:
The heavens proclaim the glory of God.
The skies display his craftsmanship.
NLT.2:
Day after day they continue to speak;
night after night they make him known.
NLT.3:
They speak without a sound or word;
their voice is never heard.
NLT.4:
Yet their message has gone throughout the earth,
and their words to all the world.
God has made a home in the heavens for the sun.
NLT.5:
It bursts forth like a radiant bridegroom after his wedding.
It rejoices like a great athlete eager to run the race.
NLT.6:
The sun rises at one end of the heavens
and follows its course to the other end.
Nothing can hide from its heat.
NLT.7:
The instructions of the LORD are perfect,
reviving the soul.
The decrees of the LORD are trustworthy,
making wise the simple.
NLT.8:
The commandments of the LORD are right,
bringing joy to the heart.
The commands of the LORD are clear,
giving insight for living.
NLT.9:
Reverence for the LORD is pure,
lasting forever.
The laws of the LORD are true;
each one is fair.
NLT.10:
They are more desirable than gold,
even the finest gold.
They are sweeter than honey,
even honey dripping from the comb.
NLT.11:
They are a warning to your servant,
a great reward for those who obey them.
NLT.12:
How can I know all the sins lurking in my heart?
Cleanse me from these hidden faults.
NLT.13:
Keep your servant from deliberate sins!
Don’t let them control me.
Then I will be free of guilt
and innocent of great sin.
NLT.14:
May the words of my mouth
and the meditation of my heart
be pleasing to you,
O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. The heavens declare the glory of God. The expanse shows his handiwork.
WEB.2: Day after day they pour out speech, and night after night they display knowledge.
WEB.3: There is no speech nor language, where their voice is not heard.
WEB.4: Their voice has gone out through all the earth, their words to the end of the world. In them he has set a tent for the sun,
WEB.5: which is as a bridegroom coming out of his room, like a strong man rejoicing to run his course.
WEB.6: His going out is from the end of the heavens, his circuit to its ends; There is nothing hidden from its heat.
WEB.7: Yahweh’s law is perfect, restoring the soul. Yahweh’s testimony is sure, making wise the simple.
WEB.8: Yahweh’s precepts are right, rejoicing the heart. Yahweh’s commandment is pure, enlightening the eyes.
WEB.9: The fear of Yahweh is clean, enduring forever. Yahweh’s ordinances are true, and righteous altogether.
WEB.10: More to be desired are they than gold, yes, than much fine gold; sweeter also than honey and the extract of the honeycomb.
WEB.11: Moreover by them is your servant warned. In keeping them there is great reward.
WEB.12: Who can discern his errors? Forgive me from hidden errors.
WEB.13: Keep back your servant also from presumptuous sins. Let them not have dominion over me. Then I will be upright. I will be blameless and innocent of great transgression.
WEB.14: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight, Yahweh, my rock, and my redeemer.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] The heavens declare the glory of God,
and the expanse ⓝ proclaims the work of his hands. ⓐ
CSB17.2:
Day after day they pour out speech;
night after night they communicate knowledge. [1]
CSB17.3:
There is no speech; there are no words;
their voice is not heard.
CSB17.4:
Their message [2] has gone out to the whole earth,
and their words to the ends of the world. ⓑ
In the heavens he has pitched a tent for the sun. ⓒ
CSB17.5:
It is like a bridegroom coming from his home;
it rejoices like an athlete running a course.
CSB17.6:
It rises from one end of the heavens
and circles to their other end; ⓓ
nothing is hidden from its heat.
CSB17.7:
The instruction of the LORD is perfect,
renewing one’s life; ⓔ
the testimony of the LORD is trustworthy,
making the inexperienced wise. ⓕ
CSB17.8:
The precepts of the LORD are right,
making the heart glad; ⓖ
the command of the LORD is radiant,
making the eyes light up. ⓗ
CSB17.9:
The fear of the LORD is pure,
enduring forever; ⓘ
the ordinances of the LORD are reliable
and altogether righteous. ⓙ
CSB17.10:
They are more desirable than gold —
than an abundance of pure gold; ⓚ
and sweeter than honey
dripping from a honeycomb. ⓛ
CSB17.11:
In addition, your servant is warned by them,
and in keeping them there is an abundant reward. ⓜ
CSB17.12:
Who perceives his unintentional sins?
Cleanse me from my hidden faults. ⓝ
CSB17.13:
Moreover, keep your servant from willful sins; ⓐ
do not let them rule me. ⓑ
Then I will be blameless
and cleansed from blatant rebellion. ⓒ
CSB17.14:
May the words of my mouth
and the meditation of my heart
be acceptable to you, ⓓ
LORD, my rock and my Redeemer. ⓔ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. The heavens are telling of the glory of God;
And the expanse [of heaven] is declaring the work of His hands.
AMP.2:
Day after day pours forth speech,
And night after night reveals knowledge.
AMP.3:
There is no speech, nor are there [spoken] words [from the stars];
Their voice is not heard.
AMP.4:
Yet their voice [in quiet evidence] has gone out through all the earth,
Their words to the end of the world.
In them and in the heavens He has made a tent for the sun,
AMP.5:
Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber;
It rejoices as a strong man to run his course.
AMP.6:
The sun's rising is from one end of the heavens,
And its circuit to the other end of them;
And there is nothing hidden from its heat.
AMP.7:
The law of the LORD is perfect (flawless), restoring and refreshing the soul;
The statutes of the LORD are reliable and trustworthy, making wise the simple.
AMP.8:
The precepts of the LORD are right, bringing joy to the heart;
The commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.
AMP.9:
The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever;
The judgments of the LORD are true, they are righteous altogether.
AMP.10:
They are more desirable than gold, yes, than much fine gold;
Sweeter also than honey and the drippings of the honeycomb.
AMP.11:
Moreover, by them Your servant is warned [reminded, illuminated, and instructed];
In keeping them there is great reward.
AMP.12: Who can understand his errors or omissions? Acquit me of hidden (unconscious, unintended) faults.
AMP.13:
Also keep back Your servant from presumptuous (deliberate, willful) sins;
Let them not rule and have control over me.
Then I will be blameless (complete),
And I shall be acquitted of great transgression.
AMP.14:
Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart
Be acceptable and pleasing in Your sight,
O LORD, my [firm, immovable] rock and my Redeemer.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God's glory is on tour in the skies,
God-craft on exhibit across the horizon.
MSG.2:
Madame Day holds classes every morning,
Professor Night lectures each evening.
MSG.3:
Their words aren't heard,
their voices aren't recorded,
MSG.4:
But their silence fills the earth:
unspoken truth is spoken everywhere.
God makes a huge dome
for the sun—a superdome!
MSG.5:
The morning sun's a new husband
leaping from his honeymoon bed,
The daybreaking sun an athlete
racing to the tape.
MSG.6:
That's how God's Word vaults across the skies
from sunrise to sunset,
Melting ice, scorching deserts,
warming hearts to faith.
MSG.7:
The revelation of God is whole
and pulls our lives together.
The signposts of God are clear
and point out the right road.
MSG.8:
The life-maps of God are right,
showing the way to joy.
The directions of God are plain
and easy on the eyes.
MSG.9:
God's reputation is twenty-four-carat gold,
with a lifetime guarantee.
The decisions of God are accurate
down to the nth degree.
MSG.10:
God's Word is better than a diamond,
better than a diamond set between emeralds.
You'll like it better than strawberries in spring,
better than red, ripe strawberries.
MSG.11:
There's more: God's Word warns us of danger
and directs us to hidden treasure.
MSG.12:
Otherwise how will we find our way?
Or know when we play the fool?
MSG.13:
Clean the slate, God, so we can start the day fresh!
Keep me from stupid sins,
from thinking I can take over your work;
Then I can start this day sun-washed,
scrubbed clean of the grime of sin.
MSG.14:
These are the words in my mouth;
these are what I chew on and pray.
Accept them when I place them
on the morning altar,
O God, my Altar-Rock,
God, Priest-of-My-Altar.

NIV.1:
Psalm 19
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
The heavens declare the glory of God;
the skies proclaim the work of his hands.
NIV.2:
Day after day they pour forth speech;
night after night they display knowledge.
NIV.3:
There is no speech or language
where their voice is not heard.
NIV.4:
Their voice goes out into all the earth,
their words to the ends of the world.
In the heavens he has pitched a tent for the sun,
NIV.5:
which is like a bridegroom coming forth from his pavilion,
like a champion rejoicing to run his course.
NIV.6:
It rises at one end of the heavens
and makes its circuit to the other;
nothing is hidden from its heat.
NIV.7:
The law of the Lord is perfect,
reviving the soul.
The statutes of the Lord are trustworthy,
making wise the simple.
NIV.8:
The precepts of the Lord are right,
giving joy to the heart.
The commands of the Lord are radiant,
giving light to the eyes.
NIV.9:
The fear of the Lord is pure,
enduring forever.
The ordinances of the Lord are sure
and altogether righteous.
NIV.10:
They are more precious than gold,
than much pure gold;
they are sweeter than honey,
than honey from the comb.
NIV.11:
By them is your servant warned;
in keeping them there is great reward.
NIV.12:
Who can discern his errors?
Forgive my hidden faults.
NIV.13:
Keep your servant also from willful sins;
may they not rule over me.
Then will I be blameless,
innocent of great transgression.
NIV.14:
May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart
be pleasing in your sight,
O Lord, my Rock and my Redeemer.

NRSVCE.1:
The heavens are telling the glory of God;
and the firmament proclaims his handiwork.
NRSVCE.2:
Day to day pours forth speech,
and night to night declares knowledge.
NRSVCE.3:
There is no speech, nor are there words;
their voice is not heard;
NRSVCE.4:
yet their voice goes out through all the earth,
and their words to the end of the world.
In the heavens he has set a tent for the sun,
NRSVCE.5:
which comes out like a bridegroom from his wedding canopy,
and like a strong man runs its course with joy.
NRSVCE.6:
Its rising is from the end of the heavens,
and its circuit to the end of them;
and nothing is hid from its heat.
NRSVCE.7:
The law of the LORD is perfect,
reviving the soul;
the decrees of the LORD are sure,
making wise the simple;
NRSVCE.8:
the precepts of the LORD are right,
rejoicing the heart;
the commandment of the LORD is clear,
enlightening the eyes;
NRSVCE.9:
the fear of the LORD is pure,
enduring forever;
the ordinances of the LORD are true
and righteous altogether.
NRSVCE.10:
More to be desired are they than gold,
even much fine gold;
sweeter also than honey,
and drippings of the honeycomb.
NRSVCE.11:
Moreover by them is your servant warned;
in keeping them there is great reward.
NRSVCE.12:
But who can detect their errors?
Clear me from hidden faults.
NRSVCE.13:
Keep back your servant also from the insolent;
do not let them have dominion over me.
Then I shall be blameless,
and innocent of great transgression.
NRSVCE.14:
Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart
be acceptable to you,
O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.

NET.1: The heavens declare the glory of God; the sky displays his handiwork.
NET.2: Day after day it speaks out; night after night it reveals his greatness.
NET.3: There is no actual speech or word, nor is its voice literally heard.
NET.4: Yet its voice echoes throughout the earth; its words carry to the distant horizon. In the sky he has pitched a tent for the sun.
NET.5: Like a bridegroom it emerges from its chamber; like a strong man it enjoys running its course.
NET.6: It emerges from the distant horizon, and goes from one end of the sky to the other; nothing can escape its heat.
NET.7: The law of the LORD is perfect and preserves one’s life. The rules set down by the LORD are reliable and impart wisdom to the inexperienced.
NET.8: The LORD’s precepts are fair and make one joyful. The LORD’s commands are pure and give insight for life.
NET.9: The commands to fear the LORD are right and endure forever. The judgments given by the LORD are trustworthy and absolutely just.
NET.10: They are of greater value than gold, than even a great amount of pure gold; they bring greater delight than honey, than even the sweetest honey from a honeycomb.
NET.11: Yes, your servant finds moral guidance there; those who obey them receive a rich reward.
NET.12: Who can know all his errors? Please do not punish me for sins I am unaware of.
NET.13: Moreover, keep me from committing flagrant sins; do not allow such sins to control me. Then I will be blameless, and innocent of blatant rebellion.
NET.14: May my words and my thoughts be acceptable in your sight, O LORD, my sheltering rock and my redeemer.
